text: Don't know where to start? Wealth, health, relationships. Only areas of life that matter. Make some sort of progress in all three every day. Doesn't matter what you do. Don't need to quantify it. Just be able to go to bed knowing you at least attempted to move the needle. All focus should be on the consistency of daily action. Forget the future. Find meaning in the process and you'll just wake up one day financially stable, not fat, and around people you hug a lot. Game has been won and you didn't even realize it. Now you get to max out whatever stats you want for fun. Enjoy forever

text:
Comparison games have changed so much. We have guys in their early 20's unable to enjoy life because they're not rich yet. Wondering if some huge figure is enough to retire. Terrified of time running out

I'm an unc now but when I was that age, I don't remember feeling that level of scarcity. The comparisons I'd make were basically talking to accomplished professionals in their 40's-50's and thinking "this is a great life they're living" and just feeling hopeful about my future because of it

Most of my anxieties involved not enjoying my job, but this general fear of getting left behind in some permanent underclass was never part of the equation. Accumulate wealth over the long term. Don't worry, you'll get there one day. Simple, attainable, proof it works. Now it feels like you need to find a way to escape reality as quickly as possible. Lock in, all or nothing

Some validity to it of course. Economy different. Social media different. But pretty crazy to see how drastically things have shifted from even just a decade ago, and will be even more crazy to see what the next decade has in store

text:
Takes balls because you're intelligent enough to know that you're always able to control your perception of things. So when those perspective shifts actually end up making you feel better, you're forced to become a more honest person

You can't blame external circumstances for your problems anymore because deep down you now know that you're full of shit. You've proven to yourself time and time again that thoughts are what dictate your reality

If you fight against this truth and continue to play the victim, your subconscious will constantly remind you that you're living a perpetual lie. Easier in the short term to just be miserable than have to take responsibility for your life. Way different story in the long term

quoted_tweet.text: Whenever something happens that you don't like, you have to train yourself to immediately ask the following question. Could this have actually happened in my favor? If you're courageous and contrarian enough to become a happier person, you'll always find a reason to believe yes

text:
In Basketball if you want to get better at defense/pace-of-play you need to play someone slightly better than you. But if you want to get better at offense you need someone slightly worse than you

This is what comment sections are for. Just shred normie arguments for sport as u try to understand what the fuck they even typed. Is an easy way to write more / hash out your own ideas.
thread:
In Basketball if you want to get better at defense/pace-of-play you need to play someone slightly better than you. But if you want to get better at offense you need someone slightly worse than you

This is what comment sections are for. Just shred normie arguments for sport as u try to understand what the fuck they even typed. Is an easy way to write more / hash out your own ideas.

The issue is on the court ur defender will be slightly worse than u and in a tiktok comment section you’re literallt dealing with a meatblob that’s sentiently closer to a cow than a human

But nonethless is good exercise. I always end up doing this on my personal but many can practice with an anon acc. Can just randomly disagree with anyone (and argue the other side) & they usually just snap

It’s also dope cuz ur forced to fit the idea in a limited character count so you have to shred what isn’t absolutely essential

Engaging in intellectual comment section warfare with the 80 IQers in limited text arenas MONSTROUSLY slept on from a training ground POV

What may also happen is the 80 IQer says the most familiar hivemind response & u actually have never thought about your own response/stance on it so it forces you to develop your own perspective. Every once in a while the simplest shit (that they accidentally wrote themselves into or probably didn’t even mean to write) will actually be a good question/rebuttal that does make you think

Fun

text:
All the psychology sauce you need/want is quite literally just found in going after ur goals. it is ver sinple

On ur way there (if u actually go), u will realize 99.99999% of whats stopping u is ur psyche. the answers r found when u just try to fix ur own shit (by going after the goal - the fix is the byproduct). U will realize u:

- cant make a decision
- cant sit down & work
- cant plan something out
- cant get urself to do a task
- too tired to function
- cant stick to 1 thing long enough
- cant research long enough to find the answer
- cant track anything & lose everything
- cant have a harsh direct conversion

Etc etc. Then ur like "so how do i actually do (thing stopping you). Then in the process of solving it u learn way more about it. U go "oh wow, this is way different than I thought". Congrats. U now know psychology.

Wild to me that the average person doesn't realize the infinite psych training ground is literally strapped to them 24/7
thread:
All the psychology sauce you need/want is quite literally just found in going after ur goals. it is ver sinple

On ur way there (if u actually go), u will realize 99.99999% of whats stopping u is ur psyche. the answers r found when u just try to fix ur own shit (by going after the goal - the fix is the byproduct). U will realize u:

- cant make a decision
- cant sit down & work
- cant plan something out
- cant get urself to do a task
- too tired to function
- cant stick to 1 thing long enough
- cant research long enough to find the answer
- cant track anything & lose everything
- cant have a harsh direct conversion

Etc etc. Then ur like "so how do i actually do (thing stopping you). Then in the process of solving it u learn way more about it. U go "oh wow, this is way different than I thought". Congrats. U now know psychology.

Wild to me that the average person doesn't realize the infinite psych training ground is literally strapped to them 24/7

U can only study/learn about something if there's a directionality attached to it. Without a target, there is no baseline/metric/foundation for comparison. there is no good or bad, up or down. U literally can't learn anything useful about anything, because you have no USE for it.

If u just try to look inside ur mind u won't find anything. Nothing there. But if u say "im going to make myself sit down and work for 20 minutes", now you get to observe ALL OF THE STUFF that is stopping you from doing that. "oh wow, this thing is fucking me up". Then u figure out how to beat it. Then when u do, congrats. u now have a piece of content 😂

Then when u find someone trying to do what u did u can say "here's what worked for me" & they may try it and actually find that it works. Pretty fucking wild if u think about it. We're all in the same exact minefield, and some people have already found the solves to the exact mine ur trying to dodge. 

What a strange fucking existence. Genuinely. Gotta fucking love life lmao. This shit is beyond insane. Imcomprehensibly insane

Guy 1: "hey here's the mental sound I told MY inner tones that allowed me to type on my robot brain to earn shekels so i can afford gas for my fleshvehicle. It's fklmdivofidpk"

guy 2: fklmdivofidpk. oh wow it worked, thanks!

Like bruh

text:
Fuck "the way it's supposed to be done". Do it how YOU THE FUCK WANNA DO IT. That is how you add SPEED to your life, and genuinely leapfrog the trajectory/progress you're making INSTANTANEOUSLY

people telling u to cold email but u can just pick up the phone & rip? Do that.

people saying "build muh slide deck" but u have better idea? do that

people saying "blah blah blah im a npc blah blah blah"? (obviously)

Ask yourself "how would I get this done as quickly as possible assuming I had zero fucking clue how other people did it". Tap into your NATURAL TENDENCIES, TRUST that GOD GAVE YOU THE FACULTIES, and fly at the target without all of the added bullshit resistance of wondering how "mUhOThERpERson" did it or wants you to do it. Who Gyves thy Phuk.

Even as a test experiment, just drop the gaymap u think u gotta follow & just pull out the dub ur way. Let the blueprint go & ask yourself "how the fuck do i wanna do this tho"

Chances are that answer is NOT like anything else you see but WILL speed you the fuck up tremendously

No rules. Game of 1.

No rules. Game of 1.

I got roasted by my HS football coaches EVERY FUCKING DAY because I was literally technique-retarded. I played basketball & just said "fuck it, i'll play football". They put me at D end, had zero clue how to do anything. But I was faster than anyone I played against, so I'd quite literally just go around them or under them. Our D-line coach was on vikings & he'd continually lose his shit at my technique, but who gave a fuck

Got the sack record

Obviously there is a discernment that has to be made here. Meaning, YES the fundamentals are absolutely essential (basically everything you want in life is just getting better than everyone else at the literal basics, because they literally don't do them) - but at the SAME time, there IS an edge in how YOU would do something naturally. God designed it all in. Ur body is a supergenius & levels u can't comprehend. it KNOWS. Now - i'm not saying "listen to it blindly" - but I AM saying "study what its telling you and try to incorporate the wisdom you're being fed INTO your technique, as opposed to completely ignoring it & gaslighting yourself because some retard in authority told you it was bad"

Sauce on their face, but your way

My writing is actually a good example of this as well. I break every grammatical "rule" and just spam random schizo ideas in capital letters with zero structure or gameplan while literally never editing anything. You just get what you get. I just get what I get, lmao. It's not polished, not strategized, not thought out - it's just dropped, even to my own surprise. But THAT is fucking fun for me, so THAT is what I do

"BUut DRop ValAuE ANd EDiDt yOuR TeXt bRo!!"

nah

The moment something makes you hate your life is the moment the question "why am I actually even doing this" comes into play & if you're good at re-framing anything, you will be able to philosophically wiggle your way out of the work if it actually doesn't absolutely fucking matter to you

That was always my biggest problem. If i literally didn't like it, I am so stubborn i quite literally just won't do it because I never needed what doing it what give me, i'd just skip the line & give that to myself, realizing it was inside of me the entire time.

Sounds "oh woohoo that's great" until you realize you're an ambitious 20-something with like 2-3 options to pursue. The ONLY THING  that ever gave me a shot was positioning my life to the point where when I asked myself "what else would I be doing rn" the answer was literally "this".

If someone gave me 5 billion dollars RIGHT NOW, i would quite literally be doing the EXACT same thing with the EXACT same people. NOTHING would change

And here is crazy part - life designing/positioning your life in that way costs literally $0. It is just strategic thinking, balls & desire. And at the VERY beginning, a lot of discovery of both yourself & the world

Much fun to be had. No point in doing some meaningless bullshit when u can just build something u fucking love

text:
First time u do anything difficult, will take way longer than u "feel" like it should (as u learn all u didn't know u didn't know)

Then 2nd time, will be tremendously faster

3rd time will be lightning

Realize if ur just starting & it's hard

Will never be that hard again
thread:
First time u do anything difficult, will take way longer than u "feel" like it should (as u learn all u didn't know u didn't know)

Then 2nd time, will be tremendously faster

3rd time will be lightning

Realize if ur just starting & it's hard

Will never be that hard again

The "false" mental model u imagine the thing 2 be doesn't include anywhere CLOSE to the actual # of variables that have 2 be "sorted" for it 2 work

As u walk down path, MORE unfolds. Then when u actually "see it" (what is ACTUALLY required) ur like "oh, there's actually a ton of shit here

This "oh shit, there's actually a ton here" feeling means you are EXACTLY where u need to be. u've found the pocket. U've broke past being sideliner & are now in the game. 

Key now is to realize the game is the game. Meaning, the shit u gotta do is the shit u gotta do. Most "see" reality & then back away. U want 2 see reality & lean IN, knowing it's just obstacles (with no thumbs), and you are human with a BRAIN (and thumbs). U'll win every time.

text: Hurry up & do the abnormal so you can then do the impossible
thread: Hurry up & do the abnormal so you can then do the impossible

text:
When you’re making shit happen it will often feel like 1 step forward 2 steps back, an ANNOYING amount of times

U just need to keep taking the unforeseen bumps 2 the chin & keep fucking pushing. continually fight for every uphill inch - over & over & over & over. You’ll be like “gawt dayum”. Who gives a fuck. Keep going.

You’ll look back after a couple dominos FINALLY fall your way in a row (and give you a tiny bit of breathing room / downhill momentum) & be monstrously glad u kept ur spirit up like u did. Whatever u r doing (assuming u actually want it), will probably require more spiritual firepower than u either anticipated or probably have ever used, but fuck it. Nothing else to do.

Very normal. Keep pushing

Very normal. Keep pushing

When ur almost there, thefe is an emotion of “leaning over it” that you’ll feel that is fantastic signal. Very different from being far away from it

It’s like ur upper body is leaning over the finish line but ur feet haven’t caught up & crossed yet & ur trying to just figure out why ur feet haven’t moved. But ur BASICALLY there. Absolute green flag emotion although it manfiests as confusion.

KEEP PUSHING

text: It's easier to innovate when you're the original of something. People copying you can only copy a snapshot of your current state. They don't know which aspects of the snapshot are essential and which are random things you'd been about to change anyway.

text:
Stanford just released a new course for this Fall: Transformers & Large Language Models by the Amidi brothers. Three videos are already available for free on YouTube. 

SYLLABUS: 
> Transformers (tokenization, embeddings, attention, architecture)
> LLM foundations (MoEs, types of decoding)
> LLM training and tuning (SFT, RL , LoRA)
> LLM evaluation (LLM/VLM-as-a-judge and best practices)
> Common tricks (RoPE, attention approximation, quantization)
> Reasoning (train/test-time scaling)
> Agentic workflows (RAG, tool calling)

If you already know this stuff, this is a good chance to refresh your mind about some of these topics, and potentially code some techniques from scratch.

Course syllabus and video links: https://t.co/6Io1nitSx3

text:
the modern world narrative doesn’t want you to be a POLYMATH. 
- “you must be an expert in AI to land high-level roles in top companies”
- “you must be the best in your job so you’re not replaced by automation”
- “resist the distraction, focus”
ignore the bs narrative. you are human, a collection of irrelevant interests that form your character. it’s not your fault that you want to be a great mathematician, musician, engineer, reader. often times, great ideas come from the intersection of these far interests and change the world. Steve Jobs wouldn’t make a giant empire if he didn’t have knowledge about engineering, design, marketing, and customer psychology.

text: Barthes: “If I had to create a god, I would lend him a “slow understanding”: a kind of drip-by-drip understanding of problems. People who understand quickly frighten me.”
